goal: Use fetched origin main when rebuilding merged intent state

summary.title: Use origin main for sync notes
summary.what: Changed sync view rebuild to scan git notes from the fetched origin main ref when available, while falling back to the configured local main branch if no remote-tracking ref exists.
summary.why: Feature branches can have a stale local main branch even after sync fetches origin/main, causing merged intents with remote notes to remain proposed locally.
summary.user_goal: Fix the case where the PBT intent showed as proposed even though its git note on main marked it merged.

decisions.rationale: sync fetches origin/main, so the rebuilt view should use that fetched state rather than a possibly stale local branch.

decisions.chose: Fall back to the configured local main branch when the remote-tracking ref is absent
decisions.rationale: Repositories without origin or before first fetch should continue to work.

rejected.alternative: Require users to switch to main and pull before sync
rejected.reason: That makes sync branch-dependent and defeats the point of fetching remote state.

followups: Consider exposing the main ref used for sync in SyncResult for easier diagnostics.

architectural_claims: Sync-derived merged state should be based on the fetched main ref, not on a potentially stale local branch.
behavioral_changes: mainline sync scans notes on refs/remotes/origin/<main_branch> when available.
mainline sync reports merged state consistently from feature branches even when local main is behind.
mainline sync falls back to the configured local main branch if no remote-tracking main ref exists.
